title: Python workspace facade source-runtime elimination

execplan_profile.projection_rule: canonical_core is authoritative for intent, scope, next action, proof, continuation, and closeout; legacy fields remain compatibility projections.

canonical_core.requested_outcome: Advance #892 by replacing generated workspace runtime facade top-level source-runtime re-exports with generated per-function lazy bindings, while preserving full-completion proof failure until source-runtime delegates are replaced by generated primitives.
canonical_core.hard_constraints: Do not claim full #892 completion; do not move package-specific runtime behavior into command-generation source; keep command-generation package-agnostic; generated outputs must be reproducible from command_package_ir and generator templates.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: Bounded implementation details for generated facade rendering, static proof wording, and validation tightening.
canonical_core.escalate_when: A correct fix requires broad runtime behavior migration, changing public command behavior, or touching memory/planning runtime blockers in this slice.
canonical_core.next_action: Render generated workspace runtime facade functions as lazy wrappers instead of top-level source-runtime imports, then update static proof to keep full completion blocked while any generated facade delegates to package-owned runtime.
proof_expectations: uv run ruff check affected generator/check/generated/test files
completion_criteria: generated/workspace/python/primitives/workspace_runtime.py no longer imports agentic_workspace.workspace_runtime_primitives at module import time.
The generated facade still makes remaining source-runtime delegation explicit per function, so follow-on primitive migration can be audited function by function.
Static proof rejects full Python generated CLI completion while generated facade wrappers still delegate to package-owned runtime helpers.
Generated freshness, static proof, Python conformance, workspace CLI tests, and affected lint pass.

goal: Advance #892 by turning the generated workspace runtime facade from a top-level source-runtime re-export into generated lazy wrapper functions, without hiding that source-runtime delegates remain.
non_goals: Do not attempt the full workspace runtime primitive migration in this slice.
Do not touch memory or planning runtime blockers in this slice.
Do not claim full Python generated CLI completion.

intent.outcome: Generated workspace runtime facade no longer performs a top-level source-runtime re-export.
intent.constraints: Keep command-generation package-agnostic and keep full-completion proof blocked while facade delegates remain.
intent.latitude: Adjust generated facade rendering, static proof, and tests inside the named scope.
intent.escalation: Escalate if full behavior migration or public command behavior changes are required.

execution.next_step: Render lazy wrappers and rerun generated proof.
execution.proof: generated freshness, static proof, Python conformance, workspace CLI tests, and affected lint

invariants: Preserve the planning contract and keep the work bounded to this plan.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: #892 Python generated CLI completion with no shipped executable CLI behavior in module source and generated target behavior derived from contracts/IR plus codegen-owned primitives.

required_continuation.activation trigger: After this slice, replace remaining generated facade delegates with generated/codegen-owned primitives function by function.

iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: continue the current milestone until the completion criteria are met

intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: Convert generated workspace facade source-runtime top-level re-export into generated lazy wrappers and keep proof honest about remaining source-runtime delegates.
intent_interpretation.review guidance: Confirm the scaffolded plan still matches the promoted item before broad implementation.

execution_bounds.max changed files: about 6 plus regenerated command package metadata if freshness requires it
execution_bounds.required validation commands: generated freshness, static proof, Python conformance, workspace CLI tests, affected ruff
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: Ask before implementing broad workspace runtime semantics, changing public command behavior, or touching memory/planning runtime blockers.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: Memory runtime blockers, planning runtime blockers, package installers, and command behavior contracts not required for lazy facade wrappers.

stop_conditions.stop when: The work no longer matches the promoted item or its completion criteria.
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: A correct fix requires changing the requested outcome or ownership boundary.
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: Implementation needs files outside the filled execution bounds.
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: The selected proof cannot demonstrate the completion criteria.

context_budget.recoverable later: Repo background, historical reviews, and deferred backlog unless compact outputs point there.
context_budget.externalize before shift: Update execution_run, proof_report, finished_run_review, and closeout_distillation before pausing.
context_budget.pre-work config pull: Use compact config/startup/summary outputs before opening raw planning or routing files.
context_budget.pre-work memory pull: Route to the narrowest relevant memory only when the task needs durable repo knowledge.
context_budget.tiny resumability note: Replace generated workspace facade top-level source import with generated per-function lazy wrappers; keep static proof failing full completion while delegates remain.
context_budget.context-shift triggers: Proof failure, scope drift, interruption, handoff, or closeout.

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: Generated workspace facade should not import source runtime at module import time.

delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Facade wrapper rendering and proof wording.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: The fix needs broad runtime semantic migration or a changed ownership boundary.

Remaining generated facade delegates are explicit per function and still block full Python completion proof.

execution_run.what happened: Changed command-generation to render generated workspace runtime facade functions as lazy per-function wrappers instead of one top-level source-runtime re-export import.
execution_run.scope touched: generated workspace runtime facade rendering, generated facade output, static proof wording, proof test, and planning state
execution_run.validations run: uv run python scripts/generate/generate_command_packages.py --check; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py --python-conformance; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py --python-docker-conformance --require-docker; make test-workspace; make lint-workspace; uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py tests/test_workspace_cli_blackbox.py tests/test_generated_command_package_proof_runner.py -q; uv run ruff check affected files
execution_run.result for continuation: generated facade no longer imports source runtime at module import time; remaining per-function source-runtime delegates still block full #892 completion
execution_run.next step: Migrate the safest workspace facade functions to generated/codegen-owned primitives, starting with defaults selection and generic JSON output.

finished_run_review.intent served: partially: removed top-level generated facade re-export, but source-runtime delegates remain
finished_run_review.config compliance: used uv run agentic-workspace and checked-in planning state
finished_run_review.misinterpretation risk: medium if lazy wrappers are mistaken for generated primitive ownership
finished_run_review.follow-on decision: continue #892 with function-by-function generated primitive migration

delegation_outcome_feedback.route skipped reason: implementation kept local because generator/proof edits were tiny and tightly coupled
delegation_outcome_feedback.expected savings: moderate future savings from read-only candidate audit
delegation_outcome_feedback.quality concern: Do not treat lazy delegates as completion; they still call package-owned runtime helpers.
delegation_outcome_feedback.decomposition adjustment: Worker audit recommends migrating defaults selection/output first, then defaults loading, then target-root resolution.

proof_report.proof achieved now: Generated facade no longer imports source runtime at module import time; static proof still rejects full completion while per-function source-runtime delegates remain.
proof_report.evidence for \"proof achieved\" state: uv run python scripts/generate/generate_command_packages.py --check; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py --python-conformance; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py --python-docker-conformance --require-docker; make test-workspace; make lint-workspace; uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py tests/test_workspace_cli_blackbox.py tests/test_generated_command_package_proof_runner.py -q; uv run ruff check affected files.

intent_satisfaction.evidence of intent satisfaction: This slice removed the top-level re-export shape but did not remove per-function source-runtime delegates.

execution_summary.outcome delivered: generated workspace runtime facade uses explicit lazy wrappers rather than a module-import-time source-runtime re-export
execution_summary.post-work posterity capture: The useful residue is the ordered next primitive candidates from the worker audit and the invariant that lazy wrappers are not generated primitive ownership; both are carried through this plan's continuation bucket and static proof wording.
execution_summary.knowledge promoted (Memory/Docs/Config): static proof wording now says generated facades delegate to package-owned runtime helpers, not merely re-export them
execution_summary.resume from: migrate defaults selection and generic JSON output wrappers to generated/codegen-owned primitives

closure_check.accepted values: Accepted values: slice status = complete|completed|bounded slice complete; larger-intent status = closed|complete|completed for archive-and-close or open|partial|unfinished for archive-but-keep-lane-open; closure decision = archive-and-close|archive-but-keep-lane-open. Prefer `agentic-planning archive-plan <plan> --prepare-closeout` before hand-editing closeout fields.
closure_check.why this decision is honest: The top-level facade re-export is gone, but source-runtime delegates remain and #892 is not complete.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: Proof passed and static proof still blocks full completion while generated facades delegate to package-owned runtime.
closure_check.reopen trigger: Generated facade regains module-import-time source runtime imports, or full completion is claimed while per-function delegates remain.

improvement_signal_review.guidance: At closeout, separate acted-on, reported-only/routed, and dismissed incidental findings; keep this compact and evidence-backed.

continuation: Migrate `_select_workspace_operation_defaults` and generic JSON output paths from facade delegates to generated/codegen-owned primitives next.
